lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 16 - Efficient Training and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning

unit.name: Final heuristics and deeper FSDP implementation details
unit.description: Ends with concrete LoRA defaults, then uses audience questions to unpack how FSDP overlaps communication with compute and why sharding policy matters.
unit.summary: The final minutes first restate the practical defaults for projects: on multi-GPU setups, use mixed precision, prefer BF16 when available, move from batch-size checks to ZeRO stage 2 or ZeRO stage 3 as needed, and if that still fails use LoRA with query/value targets, rank 8, and alpha 1 as an initial setting [ts=3191s][ts=3270s]. Audience questions then drive a deeper walk-through of an FSDP timeline diagram. The instructor explains that communication overhead can be tolerable when parameter all-gathers are prefetched during current-layer forward computation and unused layer memory is freed promptly [ts=3310s][ts=3465s]. He then explains the backward side of the same timeline: parameters for the current layer are all-gathered again, gradients are computed on each worker's data, and reduce-scatter is overlapped with backward computation where possible [ts=3483s][ts=3529s]. The final implementation caveat is that FSDP performance depends heavily on how the model is partitioned into FSDP units; grouping consecutive layers can reduce communication, while poor sharding policies can make new architectures less efficient even if the abstraction looks general [ts=3559s][ts=3666s]. In response to a final question, he clarifies that from the user's perspective weights are effectively thrown away after use and streamed back in for backward passes, even if some internal caching may exist [ts=3663s][ts=3726s].

key_points.text: Recommended initial LoRA defaults are query/value matrices, rank 8, and alpha 1 when full fine-tuning is too expensive.
FSDP can hide some communication cost by prefetching the next unit's parameters during the current forward pass.
Unused layer memory is freed during FSDP execution so VRAM is recycled as computation advances.
During backward computation, FSDP again all-gathers parameters and overlaps reduce-scatter with backpropagation where possible.
FSDP performance depends strongly on the sharding policy and how layers are grouped into FSDP units.
From the user's perspective, FSDP effectively throws away weights after use and streams them back in later, though internal caching may still exist.

Audience questions then drive a deeper walk-through of an FSDP timeline diagram. The instructor explains that communication overhead can be tolerable when parameter all-gathers are prefetched during current-layer forward computation and unused layer memory is freed promptly [ts=3310s][ts=3465s]. In response to a final question, he clarifies that from the user's perspective weights are effectively thrown away after use and streamed back in for backward passes, even if some internal caching may exist [ts=3663s][ts=3726s].

kp_catalog.name: Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP)
kp_catalog.description: Stage-3 sharding scheme that partitions parameters, gradients, and optimizer state, then gathers per-layer weights on demand during forward and backward passes. A memory-saving variant of data parallelism that shards parameters, gradients, and optimizer state across GPUs and materializes weights on demand.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP) formalizes how stage-3 sharding scheme that partitions parameters, gradients, and optimizer state, then gathers per-layer weights on demand during forward and backward passes, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: LoRA low-rank adaptation
kp_catalog.description: PEFT method that freezes base weights and learns a low-rank update for selected matrices, usually controlled by rank and scaling hyperparameters.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because loRA low-rank adaptation synthesizes pEFT method that freezes base weights and learns a low-rank update for selected matrices, usually controlled by rank and scaling hyperparameters into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: FSDP sharding policy and communication overlap
kp_catalog.description: Implementation-level idea that FSDP efficiency depends on how layers are grouped into units and how communication is overlapped with computation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fSDP sharding policy and communication overlap clarifies implementation-level idea that FSDP efficiency depends on how layers are grouped into units and how communication is overlapped with computation, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
